data.title: Tilray Medical expands in Italy with Tilray Medical Italia launch
data.description:
• Tilray Medical, a division of Tilray Brands (TLRY), announced a strategic update to its medical cannabis operations in Italy with the launch of Tilray Medical Italia, the new name of the entity formerly known as FL Group.
  • The move to Tilray Medical Italia underscores the company’s effort to build a unified medical cannabis platform across Europe while strengthening its presence in one of the region’s key regulated markets.
  • Tilray Medical Italia is expanding its footprint in Italy through a growing portfolio of medical cannabis products authorized by the Italian Ministry of Health and supported by a partnership with Molteni Farmaceutici.

data.title: Axogen stock dips after pricing $124M offering
data.description:
• Axogen (AXGN) shares fell over 7% in premarket trade on Thursday after the surgical solutions developer priced an upsized public offering of 4M shares at $31.00 per share.
  • In addition, Axogen has granted the underwriters a 30-day option to purchase up to an additional 600K shares at the public offering price.
  • Gross proceeds are expected to be around $124M, assuming no exercise of the underwriters’ option to purchase additional shares. Axogen intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for early payoff and termination of its term loan facility with Oberland Capital, working capital, capital expenditures, and other general corporate purposes.
  • The proposed offering is expected to close on January 23, 2026, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.
  • AXGN --7.3% premarket to $30.59
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Atlantic Union Bank Non-GAAP EPS of $0.97 beats by $0.11, revenue of $387.1M beats by $8.15M
data.description:
• Atlantic Union Bank press release (AUB): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.97 beats by $0.11.
  • Revenue of $387.1M (+77.2% Y/Y) beats by $8.15M.
  • At December 31, 2025, the ACL was $321.3 million, an increase of $1.3 million from the prior quarter, comprised of an allowance for loan and lease losses (“ALLL”) of $295.1 million and a reserve for unfunded commitments (“RUC”) of $26.2 million. This increase in the ACL was primarily driven by loan growth in the fourth quarter of 2025.

data.title: Target declares $1.14 dividend
data.description:
• Target (TGT) declares $1.14/share quarterly dividend, in line with previous.
  • Forward yield 4.3%
  • Payable March 1; for shareholders of record Feb. 11; ex-div Feb. 11.
  • See TGT Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: ACM Research guides 2025 revenue lower than estimates
data.description:
• ACM Research (ACMR) updated revised its 2025 revenue outlook to $885M–$900M, tightening from the prior $875M–$925M range provided in November 2025. The consensus estimates stand at $906.56M.
  • The company projects 2026 revenue at $1.08B–$1.175B (vs. consensus of $1.06B), anticipating higher growth rates. CEO Dr. David Wang cited contributions from single-wafer SPM cleaning tools, Tahoe cleaning tools, and Furnace series tools amid stable wafer fab equipment spending. 
  • Q4 and annual results are due in late February 2026.
  • The stock price traded 1.9% higher on Thursday during pre-market hours of trading.

data.title: Spain calls for creating a joint EU army amid Trump's Greenland threat - report
data.description:
Spain is calling for a joint European Union army as a deterrence measure, its foreign minister Jose Manuel Albares told Reuters, ahead of an emergency EU meeting on Thursday in response to U.S. President Trump's threat to take over Greenland. 

"A joint effort would be more efficient than 27 separate national armies," Albares said, adding that the bloc should first focus on bringing together its tangible assets to integrate its defense industry and then mobilize a coalition of the willing.

The minister asserted that the proposed joint army would not replace NATO, but would demonstrate that "Europe is not a place that will let itself be coerced militarily or economically."

Trump softened his rhetoric on Greenland on Wednesday, following talks with NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte. 

But Albares' position remains unchanged, a senior foreign official told Reuters, adding that Spain was "glad a pathway for dialogue had been opened within the NATO framework, if that pathway is confirmed."

A joint European army was first proposed in 1951 to counter the Soviet Union and ensure that German rearmament did not threaten its neighbors. But the proposal was voted down by the French parliament in 1954.

"The idea of European defense was part of the origin of the EU," Albares said. "It is up to my generation to finish this task."

data.title: Hibbett launches on DoorDash
data.description:
• Hibbett, an athletic-inspired fashion retailer owned by JD Sports Fashion (JDSPY) (JDDSF), has launched on DoorDash (DASH), bringing on-demand access of footwear, apparel, and accessories to consumers across the U.S.
  • Consumers can now shop top athletic brands directly through DoorDash, with DashPass benefits available. DashPass membership program offers $0 delivery fees and reduced service fees on eligible orders from thousands of restaurants, grocery, and convenience stores nationwide.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: GE Aerospace Non-GAAP EPS of $1.57 beats by $0.14, revenue of $11.9B beats by $700M
data.description:
• GE Aerospace press release (GE): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.57 beats by $0.14.
  • Revenue of $11.9B (+20.4% Y/Y) beats by $700M.
  • Total orders of $27.0B, +74%;
  • FY26 Guiadnce: Revenue expected in LDD vs growth of 11.27% consensus. Adj EPS of $7.10-$7.40 vs $7.12 consensus
  • Shares +1.8% PM.

data.title: EU to examine bids for Warner Bros. submitted by Netflix, Paramount simultaneously
data.description:
European competition watchdogs are set to examine bids submitted by Netflix Inc. (NFLX) and Paramount Skydance Corp. (PSKY) to acquire the coveted assets of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. (WBD) at the same time, according to a Bloomberg report from Wednesday, citing sources.

The report said the parallel examinations of the bids are now inevitable due to the timing of the rival proposals and the fact that both sides have already sounded out EU watchdogs about their plans.

Sources who spoke to Bloomberg said that a double probe would give Brussels unusual leverage over Warner’s destiny, as they could swiftly clear the deal for one bidder and launch a more in-depth investigation for the other, thus paving a path for a frontrunner to emerge eventually.

While Netflix is expected to win the bidding war for Warner's assets, its $82.7B all-cash deal is considered more complicated from an antitrust perspective due to the streaming giant’s powerful position in subscription video-on-demand across Europe.

On the other hand, Paramount recently held talks with French President Emmanuel Macron and held discussions with UK officials as part of a European charm offensive to gain support for its $108.4B hostile bid for Warner Bros.

data.title: Ray Dalio flags growing shift away from U.S. assets
data.description:
Ray Dalio, founder of Bridgewater Associates, said there has been an ongoing general diversification away from U.S. assets, particularly by global central banks.

Speaking to Bloomberg Television’s Francine Lacqua in Davos on Thursday, Dalio pointed to the sharp rise in gold prices as evidence of the shift. “When you look at gold being up 67%, it’s not a precious metal that goes up 60%,” he said. “It was bought by central banks particularly, but others, and in order to diversify fiat currencies, not just dollars.”

Dalio also said markets are paying too much attention to the trade war and not enough to what he described as a “capital war.” Referring to U.S. President Donald Trump ruling out military force in his push to acquire Greenland, Dalio said the remarks were significant. “That speech was very important because there is a line, red line,” he said, adding that any military move would carry major capital-market consequences.

“If there’s a military move, it has capital-war implications,” Dalio added.

The move away from U.S. assets has gained momentum amid rising transatlantic tensions and concerns over a potential weakening of NATO. Danish pension fund AkademikerPension has said it plans to exit U.S. Treasuries by the end of the month, citing credit risks.

“The US is basically not a good credit and long-term the US government finances are not sustainable,” Anders Schelde, chief investment officer at AkademikerPension, told Bloomberg on Tuesday. AkademikerPension, which manages around $25B in savings for academics, held about $100M in U.S. Treasuries at the end of 2025, Schelde said.

UBS Group chief executive officer Sergio Ermotti, however, has warned that the temptation to weaponize holdings of U.S. government debt is a “dangerous bet.”

U.S. Treasuries: U.S. 2-Year Bond Yield (US2Y), U.S. 5-Year (US5Y), U.S. 10-Year (US10Y), U.S. 30-Year (US30Y). Investors can also track bond ETFs here.

Major U.S. stock indices (sensitive to debt/economic concerns): (SPY), (QQQ), (DIA). Volatility indicator (VIX).
